must_not_assert: that shadow copies were successfully deleted (A0 shows the vssadmin command invocation, not its success status)
that the BCD recovery settings were successfully changed (EV-002 and EV-003 show bcdedit invocations without result or state-confirmation events)
that the System event log was successfully cleared (EV-004 shows the wevtutil invocation; only Security event 1102 in EV-001 confirms a Security-log clear)
that EV-001 was caused by the specific wevtutil process in EV-005 (the events are temporally consistent, but the package does not directly link them)

the_trap: A destructive and anti-forensics command sequence is invoked by corp\Administrator at High integrity from C:\Temp\: vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet (A0), two bcdedit commands targeting boot-recovery settings (EV-002/EV-003), and wevtutil.exe cl System and cl Security (EV-004/EV-005). These process events establish invocation and destructive intent, but not successful VSS deletion, BCD modification, or System-log clearing. Separately, Security event 1102 (EV-001) directly confirms that the Security log was cleared in the same time window, although it is not linked to a specific process event. The confirmed Security-log clear plus the coordinated privileged command chain support high/critical severity without overstating the other outcomes.
rationale: A0 shows vssadmin delete shadows /all /quiet invoked; EV-002/EV-003 show two bcdedit commands targeting boot-recovery settings; and EV-004/EV-005 show wevtutil cl System and cl Security invoked by corp\Administrator at High integrity from C:\Temp\. These process-creation events confirm the destructive and anti-forensics commands were launched, but they do not provide success status for shadow-copy deletion, BCD modification, or System-log clearing. EV-001 is a Security event 1102 record that independently confirms the Security log was cleared in the same window; the package does not directly attribute it to EV-005 or show which records were lost. The confirmed Security-log clear, reinforced by the coordinated privileged command chain, supports malicious/critical and preserve/hold-high while avoiding claims of unobserved outcomes.
